name: premature_optimization
description: Optimizing code before measuring and proving it's actually a bottleneck, often at the cost of readability and maintainability.
symptoms: Complex code without proven performance need
Spending time optimizing rarely-used code paths
Using clever tricks instead of clear code
consequences: Harder to read and maintain code
Harder to optimize the actual bottlenecks

famous_quote.text: Premature optimization is the root of all evil
famous_quote.author: Donald Knuth
famous_quote.context: 97% of the time; we should forget about small efficiencies

bad_example.description: Over-optimized before knowing if it matters

legitimate_early_optimizations: Choosing appropriate data structures (O(n) vs O(1) lookup)
Database indexing for known query patterns
prevention: Set performance budgets and test against them
Keep original readable version in comments if complex
